evidences.title: Trading Tesla stock around earnings is a coin toss, but long-term investors have better odds
evidences.content: ... Yahoo Finance # Trading Tesla stock around earnings is a coin toss, but long-term investors have better odds Jared Blikre Jared Blikre 2 min read TSLA -2.19% Tesla's post-earnings reaction can look like a trader's dream — or a trap. The stock has a habit of making outsized moves after results, but the historical record shows those short-term bets are close to a toss-up. For investors, the better roadmap has usually been patience. That pattern shows up clearly in the data. Tesla: Median returns for different holding periods after buying before earnings Since 2010, buying Tesla (TSLA) just before quarterly earnings and holding for only one day has produced a median return of -1%, with a win rate, which refers to the percentage of the time the return was positive, of 48%. [...] But that expected move was still well below the stock's average post-earnings move of 11% over the last 10 reports — a reminder that Tesla's first move after results can be far more violent than a normal session. And the recent history does little to make that first-day trade look easier. Over Tesla's last 10 earnings reactions, the stock has finished higher five times and lower five times. The average w...

evidences.content: or tomorrow's options with tomorrow's options implied volatility. And first I'm going to break this down for you. So options implied volatility measures investors expectations of future price swings based on options prices. And here we have that number all the way to the left here. Options implied volatility 10.7%. So options traders are expecting the Wednesday close and then the announcement. So Wednesday close to Thursday, that range would be 10.7% and it doesn't tell you if it's going to be up or down, just the range. And then here's what happened over the last two and a half years. The actual reaction was about 12.8%. So this tells us that options traders are expecting a little bit less volatility than we've seen over the last two and a half two and a half years on average. evidences.title: Tesla Releases Q1 2026 Earnings Estimates from Analysts - Not a Tesla App
evidences.content: ## Setting the Stage for Q1 Results This earnings report follows a quarter in which Tesla's delivery numbers came in slightly below analyst projections. The company delivered 358,023 vehicles, just missing the initial analyst consensus of 365,645. Even with the slight miss, deliveries grew about 6.3% compared to the first quarter of last year. To put these new earnings estimates in perspective, we can look back at Q1 2025. In that first quarter of last year, Tesla reported an adjusted EPS of $0.27 and revenue of $19.34 billion. While the Q1 estimates show a healthy gain in earnings that aligns with the year-over-year growth in deliveries, the focus during the call will likely be on margins and future growth rather than just the raw revenue numbers. [...] Not a Tesla App # Tesla Releases Q1 2026 Earnings Estimates from Analysts ## Tesla is gearing up for its first major financial check-in of the year. The company has officially scheduled its Q1 2026 earnings call for after the bell on Wednesday, April 22, 2026. evidences.content: ## Consensus Estimates vs. Year-Ago Results | Metric | Q1 2026 Estimate | Q1 2025 Actual | YoY Change | --- --- | | Non-GAAP EPS | $0.33 | $0.12 | +175% | | Revenue | ~$21.4B | $19.335B | ~+10.7% | | Full Year EPS (2026E) | ~$2.56 | $1.66 (FY2025 actual) | ~+54% | | Full Year Revenue (2026E) | ~$107B | $94.827B (FY2025 actual) | ~+13% | Consensus estimates sourced from Tesla’s company-compiled analyst consensus, aggregating 20 sell-side analysts. Full-year figures are analyst forecasts, not company guidance. ## Margins, Energy, and the FSD Inflection [...] ## Margins, Energy, and the FSD Inflection Gross margin is the most critical metric to watch. Tesla rebuilt automotive gross margin to 20.4% in Q4 2025 (17.9% excluding regulatory credits), but tariff complexity and elevated operating expenses remain real pressures. Operating expenses surged 50% year over year in Q3 2025 to $3.43B, and AI and R&D spending have not slowed. If margins hold or expand despite those headwinds, that would be a strong signal. evidences.title: Tesla stock shows this curious pattern before and after earnings that you can trade - MarketWatch
evidences.content: Advanced Search➔ Search Results Symbols No results found All News Articles Video Podcasts 0Results No Results Found Authors No results found Sections No results found Columns No results found 1. Home 2. Investing 3. Stocks 4. Mark Hulbert ## Mark Hulbert # Tesla stock shows this curious pattern before and after earnings that you can trade ## Shares typically decline during the two weeks ahead of earnings and gain over the following two weeks Updated Tesla is scheduled to report quarterly earnings July 17. Tesla is scheduled to report quarterly earnings July 17. Photo: Getty Images [...] Over the past 12 earnings cycles, Tesla’s stock in the two weeks after it reported earnings has performed close to 10 percentage points better than in the two weeks prior, on average. (Specifically, the shares gained 6.7% on average in the post-earnings two-week period, versus minus-2.9% over the two weeks prior.) These are raw, unannualized numbers; on an annualized basis the difference is huge. The difference between the S&P 500’s average return in these before-and-after periods is essentially zero, so what the chart is showing is not a marketwide phenomenon but specific to Tesla. evidences.content: ## Tangent Elon Musk, Tesla’s billionaire CEO and largest shareholder, is amidst a particularly busy stretch at one of his other gigs as Twitter’s CEO. Musk finalized the renaming of Twitter to X, the name of his everything app and made a series of surprising comments in an interview that aired Monday, including accusing Meta’s billionaire CEO Mark Zuckerberg of tilting the 2020 election toward Democrats, confirming Twitter is worth half of what he paid for it last year and sharing he wants to build his own generative artificial intelligence product. ## Further Reading Tesla Shares Drop as Musk Cuts Prices Again Ahead of Earnings (Bloomberg) Tesla’s Quarterly EV Deliveries Jump 36%, Buoyed By Price Cuts, Federal Incentives (Forbes) [...] Breaking Business # Here’s How Tesla’s Stock Performed After Its Last 10 Earnings Reports ByDerek Saul, Former Staff. evidences.content: AI-generated summary ## Introduction Tesla (TSLA) is scheduled to release its first-quarter 2026 earnings report after the U.S. market close on April 22. The Non-GAAP EPS consensus from Tesla's official compilation (comprising 20 major sell-side analysts from firms like Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and Wedbush) stands at $0.33 with revenue around $21.4 billion; meanwhile, third-party institutional aggregators such as Bloomberg and Refinitiv place estimates between $0.37 to $0.40 for EPS and $22 to $23 billion for revenue. The significant discrepancy between these sources reflects a fundamental divergence in analyst judgment regarding the company's fundamentals. [...] Ahead of the earnings report, the market has already received two key leading datasets: actual Q1 deliveries were 358,000 units, approximately 7,600 fewer than the expected 365,600; energy storage deployment nearly halved from the historical high of 14.2 GWh in Q4 2025 to 8.8 GWh, a 38% sequential decline.
